title: Build the Right Customer Structure
description: Understand how to structure customer accounts and relationships for maximum commercial success

content.title: Building Effective Account Structures
content.subtitle: The foundation of long-term customer success

content.text: A properly structured customer account creates clarity around decision-making, budget ownership, and strategic alignment. This section explores how to identify the right organizational structure within your customer accounts and map it to your commercial strategy.

stages.label: 1. Map Organizational Chart
stages.description: Understand the customer's internal organizational structure and reporting lines

stages.label: 2. Identify Decision Makers
stages.description: Locate budget holders, influencers, and technical decision makers

stages.label: 3. Define Account Hierarchy
stages.description: Structure parent/child accounts based on customer geography or business units

stages.label: 4. Align to Strategy
stages.description: Connect account structure to your commercial and customer success plans

mistakes.text: Flat account structures with no hierarchy
mistakes.whyHarmful: Makes it difficult to manage multi-division accounts and creates visibility gaps
Leads to surprised stakeholders and delayed approvals later in the sales cycle

mistakes.text: Not updating structure as organization changes
mistakes.whyHarmful: Creates stale data and missed opportunities when customers reorganize

content.title: Lab: Account Structure Assessment
content.description: Assess and restructure a complex customer account

items: Clear account structures enable better territory and account management
Map both reporting lines and budget ownership separately
Align account structure with customer success and expansion plans
Regular account reviews catch organizational changes early

keyTakeaways: Clear account structures enable better territory and account management
Map both reporting lines and budget ownership separately
Align account structure with customer success and expansion plans
Regular account reviews catch organizational changes early
